steps.title: Identify the contextual signal
steps.content: The text says certain features are "almost always included in the designs of mosques" — the minaret is a defining, characteristic element of mosque architecture. The blank needs a word meaning a distinctive identifying feature.

steps.title: Match the precise word
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean a defining characteristic — something that marks mosque architecture as distinctly itself. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — ‘hallmark’: "considered to be a hallmark of mosque architecture" — hallmark means a distinguishing characteristic or signature feature, exactly fitting a standard element that defines the style. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘rejection’: "considered to be a rejection of mosque architecture" — rejection means a refusal or denial; the minaret is embraced by mosque design, not rejected. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘motivation’: "considered to be a motivation of mosque architecture" — motivation means a reason for doing something; a minaret is a structural feature, not a reason for building. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘duplicate’: "considered to be a duplicate of mosque architecture" — duplicate means an exact copy; the minaret is an original element, not a copy of something else. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
